microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory food THEN 2 min BECAUSE they likely want it warm without overcooking. IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND physically fatigued AND refrigerated savory food THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and hotter food. IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory food THEN 1 min BECAUSE speed matters more than ideal warmth. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are intended cold. IF social setting with a partner on either side AND neutral AND refrigerated savory food THEN 2 min BECAUSE they keep a standard warm meal in social contexts. IF watching TV or working in front AND relaxed with ample time AND refrigerated savory food THEN 3 min BECAUSE they tolerate extra prep time for a hotter meal. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND refrigerated dessert or brownie THEN 1 min BECAUSE they still want slight warming but prioritize keeping the meal moving.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND neutral AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE there is no competing visual target. IF social setting with a person to the left AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE preserving sightline to the partner matters. IF social setting with a person to the right AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE they want to keep that interaction channel open. IF watching television in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the screen is the main competing focus. IF working on a laptop or tablet in front AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE task visibility is more important than shortest robot motion. IF social setting on one side AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion or minimize right occlusion BECAUSE this user's tie-break favors maintaining social visibility over efficiency even when tired. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE efficiency wins when no one and nothing important is being blocked.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND neutral AND any food THEN medium BECAUSE they want efficient but not abrupt motion. IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE fatigue increases desire for comfort and predictability. IF any social setting AND physically fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE this user's tie-break favors comfort over speed under fatigue. IF any context AND relaxed with ample time AND messy or saucy foods THEN slow BECAUSE careful handling reduces perceived mess and increases comfort.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone in a quiet setting AND neutral AND bite-sized or elongated foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE limited mouth opening makes narrower bites easier. IF any dining context AND any affective state AND large or tough meats THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE mouth-opening limits outweigh acquisition convenience. IF any dining context AND physically fatigued AND any forkable food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE easier oral access matters more when tired. IF any dining context AND in a hurry AND slippery food with repeated acquisition failures THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this user will trade bite narrowness for success after failures. IF social setting AND neutral AND foods prone to falling off the fork THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding repeated failed attempts is less awkward socially. IF watching TV or working in front AND neutral AND standard foods THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the stable baseline favors easier eating. IF any context AND dessert or soft fruit pieces THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE success is usually adequate and smaller bites remain preferable.
